justification: A is better overall due to more detailed CoT that at least considers the graphene bands and provides clearer analysis steps, despite both being fundamentally wrong on the core classification.

step1.A: Incorrect - misidentified as Si, noted but dismissed G/2D
step1.B: Incorrect - misidentified as Si, ignored G/2D

step2.A: Structured and logical for Si but misses graphene physics; acknowledges extra peaks
step2.B: Superficial, lacks depth and band assignments

step3.A: Good format and peak list but wrong material, no ratios or FWHM
step3.B: Similar format but even less explanatory

thinking: Both A and B completely failed Step 1 by classifying the spectrum as bulk crystalline silicon based on the dominant 522 cm⁻¹ peak, ignoring or dismissing the clear G (~1580 cm⁻¹) and 2D (~2670 cm⁻¹) bands that match the graphene ground truth. Their CoT reasoning is internally consistent for silicon but scientifically flawed and ungrounded in the provided graphene context. A is marginally more complete because it explicitly acknowledges the 1579/2670 cm⁻¹ peaks (even if it wrongly dismisses them), while B is shallower and never addresses them.
